user_query:
I need to document contamination trends on my vineyard for an attorney. Please analyze the provided soil sampling data to detect trends and regulatory exceedances, limited to active vineyard parcels. Use these inputs:
- input/soil_samples.csv (sample-level results)
- input/regulatory_limits.csv (contaminant limits)
- input/parcel_info.csv (parcel status)

Requirements and filters:
1) Only include records where qa_flag = "OK", sample_type = "soil", and the parcel has planted_status = "active" per input/parcel_info.csv.
2) Treat exceedances as value_mgkg strictly greater than limit_mgkg (not equal-to).
3) Define calendar quarters as Q1=Jan–Mar, Q2=Apr–Jun, Q3=Jul–Sep, Q4=Oct–Dec.

Deliverables (all CSVs) under the outputs/ directory:
A) outputs/quarterly_means.csv — Quarterly aggregates per parcel_id and contaminant using the filtered dataset, with columns:
   - parcel_id, contaminant, year, quarter, mean_mgkg, n_samples_in_quarter
B) outputs/exceedances.csv — Sample-level exceedances after filtering, sorted by exceedance_mgkg (descending). Columns:
   - sample_id, parcel_id, sample_date, contaminant, value_mgkg, limit_mgkg, exceedance_mgkg (value_mgkg - limit_mgkg)
C) outputs/parcel_trends.csv — Parcel-level trend summary per contaminant (active parcels only), computed from the filtered dataset. Columns:
   - parcel_id, contaminant, n_samples_used, first_sample_date, last_sample_date, first_value_mgkg (chronological), last_value_mgkg, delta_mgkg (last - first), mean_mgkg_overall, quarters_total, quarters_above_limit
D) outputs/top_rising_parcels.csv — For each contaminant, rank the top 3 active parcels by largest positive delta_mgkg from outputs/parcel_trends.csv, restricted to parcels with at least 3 samples for that contaminant. Include:
   - contaminant, rank (1..n), parcel_id, n_samples_used, delta_mgkg, mean_exceedance_mgkg_over_limit (average of (value_mgkg - limit_mgkg) for that parcel/contaminant over only the exceeding samples; if none exceed, this should be 0). Sort by contaminant (A–Z), then rank ascending. If fewer than 3 parcels qualify for a contaminant, list only those that qualify.

Notes:
- All statistics and rankings must be computed after applying the filters in (1).
- Date parsing is ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) as provided.
- Please ensure the deliverables reflect both the aggregate statistics and the filtered, sorted/ranked views so my legal team can quickly see where contaminants are rising and where limits are exceeded. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
